Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: From the prompt, a process variant is the ordered sequence of activities for a single case, sorted by timestamp.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: DuckDB has the LIST aggregate function which can collect values in order.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: for each case_id, group by case_id, order by timestamp, and then use LIST(activity ORDER BY timestamp) to get the sequence.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 1. For each case_id, compute the variant as a list. 2. Count the frequency of each variant. 3. Find the top K variants highest frequencies. 4. Get all case_ids that have variants in the top K. 5. Select all events from event_log where case_id is in that set.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: WITH case_variants AS ( SELECT case_id, LIST(activity ORDER BY timestamp) AS variant FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id ), variant_frequencies AS ( SELECT variant, COUNT(*) AS frequency FROM case_variants GROUP BY variant ORDER BY frequency DESC LIMIT K )

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: K is a parameter, in the query have ORDER BY frequency DESC LIMIT K

Name: Validation - PE
Text: testing with an example Suppose K=2 Case 1 A B C Case 2 A B C Case 3 X Y Case 4 X Y Case 5 A B variants ABC 2 XY 2 AB 1 top 2 select cases 1 2 3 4 Yes.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: LIST function returns an array, comparing arrays for equality works as expected.

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Another way to write it more concise by inlining the subqueries.
